verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (trans-Golgi network localizing protein 2) is a Golgi-resident membrane protein. Canonical literature and UniProt annotation place it at the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and early endosomes, with no documented plasma membrane residency or trafficking to the outer leaflet under baseline or stressed conditions. No cell-surface-directed therapeutics, no TM-precursor shedding, no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-display, no narrow-lineage restricted surface pool, no lysosomal-exocytosis link, and no stable PM-partner anchoring. The protein lacks intrinsic surface accessibility pathways.
